sourceTitle: A Notion guide on switching between work and personal accounts

questions.question: What is one benefit of using multiple workspaces in Notion?

options.text: It allows separation of work and personal tasks
options.explanation: Correct – the guide highlights that multiple workspaces let you keep work and personal content separate, supporting a healthier work/life balance.
Wrong – workspaces do not affect storage limits; they are for organizing content.
Wrong – offline mode is a separate feature and not tied to the number of workspaces.

options.text: It automatically syncs with Google Calendar
options.explanation: Wrong – syncing with Google Calendar requires an integration, not simply creating multiple workspaces.

questions.question: Which platforms support Notion's account‑switching feature?

options.explanation: Incorrect – the guide states the feature works on both desktop and mobile.
Incorrect – it is not limited to mobile devices.
Correct – the tutorial mentions the account‑switching capability is available on desktop and mobile platforms.
Incorrect – while the web version works, the guide specifically calls out desktop and mobile apps.

questions.question: How does the guide suggest you begin organizing with Notion templates?

options.text: By creating a template from scratch
options.explanation: Wrong – although you can build your own, the guide points you to the existing template gallery.

options.text: By browsing the template gallery that contains over 10,000 templates
options.explanation: Correct – the guide explicitly encourages users to start by browsing the extensive template gallery.

options.text: By importing templates from Google Docs
options.explanation: Incorrect – importing from Google Docs is not mentioned as a starting point.
Incorrect – AI‑generated templates are not referenced in the article.

questions.question: What primary purpose does the guide give for adding another account to your Notion app?

options.explanation: Wrong – adding an account does not affect upload limits.

options.text: To enable collaboration with external teams
options.explanation: Incorrect – while collaboration is possible, the guide emphasizes personal vs. work separation.

options.text: To separate work and personal spaces for a better work/life balance
options.explanation: Correct – the guide states that multiple accounts help keep work and personal content distinct, supporting balance.
Wrong – adding an account is unrelated to premium feature activation.

questions.question: If an ad blocker stops the tutorial video from playing, where does the guide recommend you watch it?

options.explanation: Incorrect – Vimeo is not mentioned as an alternative.
Correct – the article provides a direct YouTube link for the video.
Wrong – the internal player is blocked by the ad blocker.

options.text: Download it as an MP4 file
options.explanation: Incorrect – no download option is offered in the guide.

questions.question: Which of the following resources is NOT listed at the end of the guide?

options.explanation: Incorrect – the template gallery is referenced earlier in the guide.
Incorrect – a link to the help center is provided.
Incorrect – the guide includes a link to subscribe on YouTube.
Correct – the guide does not mention a community forum among its resources.

questions.question: Considering the guide’s focus on work/life balance, which practice best utilizes Notion’s account‑switching feature?

options.text: Use a single workspace for all tasks to minimize context switching
options.explanation: Wrong – this defeats the purpose of separating work and personal content.

options.text: Create separate workspaces for work and personal tasks and switch accounts daily
options.explanation: Correct – the guide recommends distinct workspaces and easy switching to keep boundaries clear.

options.text: Share the same account across all devices without separate workspaces
options.explanation: Incorrect – sharing one account blurs the work/personal line the guide aims to preserve.

options.text: Disable account switching to focus on a single project
options.explanation: Wrong – disabling the feature removes the balance benefit highlighted in the guide.
